stemHtml: A student pilot is flying with an instructor in a single engine aircraft. The instructor is demonstrating the effects of a stall shortly after take-off, specifically the effects of a stall during a turn. What are the initial effects of the stall in this scenario, and correct recovery procedures?

options.a: The slipstream covers up the buffeting, so reduce power in order to better diagnose stall symptoms.
options.b: The right or the left wing might drop depending on aircraft design, lower the nose and use rudder to stop any roll if necessary.
options.c: Reduce power, roll wings level and perform a normal power-off stall recovery.
options.d: The outer wing may drop, roll wings level to increase elevator authority before pushing the control column forward.
